title: QA: Bash hook command injection via unescaped shell interpolation (hooks/mod.rs)
description:
## Severity
Critical
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/hooks/mod.rs:647-673
## Violation
`run_command_hook` shells out via `sh -c <command>` with no escaping or
allowlisting:
```rust
let (shell, shell_arg) = if cfg!(windows) {
    ("cmd", "/C")
} else {
    ("sh", "-c")
};
let mut child = Command::new(shell)
    .arg(shell_arg)
    .arg(command)
```
While `command` is sourced from a HookEntry, that HookEntry can be loaded
from layered settings files including `.claude/settings.local.json` and
`/etc/openclaudia/managed-settings.json` (see `claude_compat.rs:108-184`).
Any code path that ever lets untrusted content into a settings layer gains
arbitrary shell execution. There is no separation between "trusted config"
and "command to run"; the entire surface area is monolithic shell text.
Additionally the env var `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` is set to
`std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_default()` which on failure becomes the
empty path — silently masking misconfiguration.
## Mandated refactor
Split hook execution into two explicit modes: (1) a `Command::new(argv0).args(argv_tail)`
exec-style hook taking a parsed argv vector (no shell), and (2) a `Hook::Shell`
variant that is only enabled when an explicit `allow_shell: true` field is
set in the hook entry. Reject shell hooks loaded from non-project layers by
default (managed/user-global). Refuse to launch when `current_dir()` fails
rather than passing an empty `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
